requirements.full_sentence:
Some of the Rural Cooperative Banks (RCBs) may also be involved in implementation
of various forms of Debt Relief Schemes (DRS) announced by State Governments
that inter alia entail sacrifice / waiver of debt obligations of a targeted segment of
borrowers, against fiscal support.
requirements.object: implementation of various forms of Debt Relief Schemes (DRS) announced by State Governments that inter alia entail sacrifice / wai

conditions: If such schemes are announced frequently
requirements.full_sentence:
If such schemes are announced frequently,
incommensurately, or without due consideration to the principles of financial discipline,
they would negatively affect credit discipline and in the long run, may be counter-
productive to the credit flow to such borrowers.
requirements.object: the credit flow to such borrowers

requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative
Banks - Resolution of Stressed Assets) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Resolution of Stressed Assets) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall come into force with immediate effect unless specified
otherwise.
requirements.object: immediate effect unless specified otherwise

requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be applicable to Rural Co-operative Banks (hereinafter
collectively referred to as 'banks' and individually as a 'bank').
requirements.object: Co-operative Banks (hereinafter collectively referred to as 'banks' and individually as a 'bank')

requirements.full_sentence:
In this context, rural co-operative banks shall mean State Co-operative Banks and
Central Co-operative Banks, as defined in the National Bank for Agriculture and
Rural Development Act, 1981.
requirements.object: and Central Co-operative Banks, as defined in the National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development Act, 1981

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall follow the requirements of the State Co-operative Societies Acts and /
or rules made thereunder, or other statutory enactments, if they are more stringent
than those prescribed hereby.
requirements.object: the State Co-operative Societies Acts and / or rules made thereunder, or other statutory enactments

requirements.full_sentence:
In these Directions, the following definitions shall apply, unless the context otherwise
requires:
(1) 'compromise settlement' shall refer to any negotiated arrangement with the
borrower to fully settle the claims of a bank against the borrower in cash.

Explanation: Compromise settlement may entail some sacrifice of the
amount due from the borrower on the part of the bank with corresponding
waiver of claims of the bank against the borrower to that extent.
requirements.object: the amount due from the borrower on the part of the bank with corresponding waiver of claims of the bank against the borrower

requirements.full_sentence:
1(1A) 'date of invocation' for the purpose of Chapter IV-A of these Directions
shall mean the date on which the borrower and the bank agree to proceed
with a resolution plan through a documented arrangement, other than in
case of deemed invocation as specified in paragraph 33O of these
Directions.
requirements.object: which the borrower and the bank agree to proceed with a resolution plan through a documented arrangement, other than in case of deeme

conditions:
when whole or any part or instalment of the
1 Inserted with effect from July 01
requirements.full_sentence:
(2) 'default' shall mean non-payment of debt (as defined under the Insolvency
and Bankruptcy Code, 2016) when whole or any part or instalment of the
1 Inserted with effect from July 01, 2026 vide Reserve Bank of India (Rural Cooperative Banks - Resolution of Stressed Assets) Amendment Directions, 2026 dated April 29, 2026.
requirements.object: (as defined under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016) when whole or any part or instalment of the 1 Inserted with effec

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall put in place a Board-approved policy for restructuring / rehabilitation of
stressed assets.
requirements.object: Board-approved policy for restructuring / rehabilitation of stressed assets

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall put in place Board-approved policies for undertaking compromise
settlements with the borrowers as well as for technical write-offs, which shall inter
alia include the following:
(1) comprehensive prescription of the process to be followed for all compromise
settlements and technical write-offs, with specific guidance on the necessary
conditions precedent such as minimum ageing, deterioration in collateral
value etc.;
requirements.object: policies for undertaking compromise settlements with the borrowers as well as for technical write-offs, which shall inter

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) graded framework for examination of staff accountability in such cases with
reasonable thresholds and timelines as may be decided by the Board;
requirements.object: Board

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) delegation of power for such approvals rests with an authority
(individual or committee, as the case may be) which is at least one
level higher in hierarchy than the authority vested with power to
sanction the credit / investment exposure.
requirements.object: least one level higher in hierarchy than the authority vested with power to sanction the credit / investment exposure

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that any official who was part of sanctioning the loan (as
individual or part of a committee) shall not be part of the approving the
<image>
proposal for compromise settlement of the same loan account, in any
capacity.
requirements.object: approving the <image> proposal for compromise settlement of the same loan account, in any capacity

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) proposals for compromise settlements in respect of borrowers
classified as fraud or wilful defaulter, as permitted in terms of
paragraphs 17 and 32, shall require approval of the Board in all cases.
requirements.object: Board in all cases

requirements.full_sentence:
The board approved policy of the bank shall incorporate provisions for resolution
as provided for under Chapter IV-A of these Directions, including the following:
(1) the objective principles for the terms of relief to be granted to various borrower / loan
categories.
requirements.object: as provided for under Chapter IV-A of these Directions, including the following: (1) the objective principles fo

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall recognise incipient stress in loan accounts, immediately on default, by
classifying such assets as special mention accounts (SMA) as per the following
categories:
Loans in the nature of revolving facilities like
Loans other than revolving facilities
cash credit/overdraft
Basis for classification -
Outstanding balance remains continuously in excess of the sanctioned
Basis for classification - Principal or interest payment or
SMA Sub- categories
SMA Sub- categories
any other amount wholly or
limit or drawing power, whichever is lower, for a
partly overdue
period of
SMA-0 Up to 30 days
More than 30 days and up
SMA-1 More than 30 days and up to 60
days SMA-1
to 60 days
3 Inserted with effect from July 01, 2026 vide Reserve Bank of India (Rural Cooperative Banks - Resolution of Stressed Assets) Amendment Directions, 2026 dated April 29, 2026.
requirements.object: loan accounts, immediately on default, by classifying such assets as special mention accounts (SMA) as per the following

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall adhere to the relevant provisions on submission of financial information
to information utilities of Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016 and Insolvency and
Bankruptcy Board of India (Information Utilities) Regulations, 2017 and immediately
put in place appropriate systems and procedures to ensure compliance to the
provisions of the Code and Regulations.
requirements.object: provisions on submission of financial information to information utilities of Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016 and Insolven

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall make suitable disclosures in its financial statements in the 'Notes to
Accounts', as specified in the Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks -
Financial Statements: Presentation and Disclosures) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: its financial statements in the 'Notes to Accounts', as specified in the Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks

requirements.full_sentence:
An asset, where the terms and conditions of the loans regarding payment of interest
and repayment of principal have been renegotiated or rescheduled, shall be
classified as sub-standard and shall remain so in such category for at least one year
of satisfactory performance under the renegotiated or rescheduled terms.
requirements.object: and shall remain so in such category for at least one year of satisfactory performance under the renegotiated or resched

requirements.full_sentence:
The classification of an asset shall not be upgraded merely as a result of
rescheduling unless there is satisfactory compliance of condition specified at
paragraph 13 above.
requirements.object: a result of rescheduling unless there is satisfactory compliance of condition specified at paragraph 13 above

requirements.full_sentence:
Borrowers who have committed frauds/ malfeasance/ wilful default as well as any
entity with which a wilful defaulter is associated with, shall remain ineligible for
restructuring.

A wilful defaulter or any entity with which a wilful defaulter is associated shall be
eligible for restructuring subsequent to removal of the name of wilful defaulter from
the List of Wilful Defaulters, subject to penal measures applicable to borrowers
classified as wilful defaulter in terms of the Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-
operative Banks - Treatment of Wilful Defaulters and Large Defaulters) Directions,
2025.
requirements.object: subsequent to removal of the name of wilful defaulter from the List of Wilful Defaulters

requirements.full_sentence:
The objective of compromise settlements shall be to maximise the possible recovery
from a distressed borrower at minimum expense, in the best interest of the bank.
requirements.object: possible recovery from a distressed borrower at minimum expense, in the best interest of the bank

requirements.full_sentence:
The compromise settlements and technical write-offs shall be without prejudice to
any mutually agreed contractual provisions between a bank and a borrower relating
to future contingent realizations or recovery by the bank, subject to such claims not
being recognised in any manner on the balance sheet of the bank at the time of the
settlement or subsequently till actual realization of such receivables.
requirements.object: any mutually agreed contractual provisions between a bank and a borrower relating to future contingent realizations or recovery by the bank

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that any such claims recognised on the balance sheet of the bank shall
render the arrangement to be treated as restructuring.
requirements.object: be treated as restructuring

requirements.full_sentence:
Notwithstanding paragraph 19, compromise settlements where the time for payment
of the agreed settlement amount exceeds three months shall be treated as
restructuring.

Any arrangement involving part settlement with the borrower shall also fall under the
definition of restructuring and shall be governed by the provisions applicable thereto.
requirements.object: definition of restructuring and shall be governed by the provisions applicable thereto

requirements.full_sentence:
However,
such technical write-offs do not entail any waiver of claims against the borrower and
thus the bank's right to recovery shall not be undermined in any manner.
requirements.object: manner

conditions: In case of partial technical write-offs
requirements.full_sentence:
In case of partial technical write-offs, the prudential requirements in respect of
residual exposure, including provisioning and asset classification, shall be with
reference to the original exposure.
requirements.object: the original exposure

requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
Provided that the amount of provision including the amount representing partial
technical write-off shall meet the extant provisioning requirements, as computed on
the gross value of the asset.
requirements.object: requirements, as computed on the gross value of the asset

requirements.full_sentence:
There shall be a reporting mechanism to the next higher authority, at least on a
quarterly basis, with respect to compromise settlements and technical write offs
approved by a particular authority.
requirements.object: to the next higher authority, at least on a quarterly basis, with respect to compromise settlements and technical write offs

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that compromise settlements and technical write-offs approved by the MD
& CEO / Board Level Committee shall be reported to the Board.
requirements.object: Board

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall mandate a suitable reporting format so as to ensure adequate
coverage of the following aspects at the minimum:
(1) trend in number of accounts and amounts subjected to compromise
settlement and/or technical write-off (q-o-q and y-o-y);
requirements.object: format so as to ensure adequate coverage of the following aspects at the minimum: (1) trend in number of accounts and amo

requirements.full_sentence:
In respect of borrowers subject to compromise settlements, there shall be a cooling
period as determined by the respective Board approved policies before the bank can
assume fresh exposures to such borrowers.
requirements.object: as determined by the respective Board approved policies before the bank can assume fresh exposures to such borrowers

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that the cooling period in respect of exposures other than farm credit
exposures shall be subject to a floor of 12 months with a bank being free to stipulate
higher cooling periods in terms of their Board approved policies.
requirements.object: floor of 12 months with a bank being free to stipulate higher cooling periods in terms of their Board approved policies

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided further that the cooling period for farm credit exposures shall be
determined by a bank as per their respective Board approved policies.
requirements.object: bank as per their respective Board approved policies

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: Farm credit for the above purpose shall refer to credit extended to
agricultural activities as listed in the Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative
requirements.object: to agricultural activities as listed in the Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative

requirements.full_sentence:
The cooling period to be adopted in respect of exposures subjected to technical
write-offs shall be as per the Board approved policies of a bank.
requirements.object: Board approved policies of a bank

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank may undertake compromise settlements or technical write-offs in respect of
accounts categorised as wilful defaulters or fraud without prejudice to the criminal
proceeding underway against such borrowers.
requirements.object: technical write-offs in respect of accounts categorised as wilful defaulters or fraud without prejudice to the cri

conditions:
where a bank enter into compromise
settlement with such borrowers
requirements.full_sentence:
The penal measures applicable to borrowers classified as fraud or wilful defaulter in
terms of the Reserve Bank of India (Fraud Risk Management in UCBs / StCBs /
CCBs) Directions, 2024 and the Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks -
Treatment of Wilful Defaulters and Large Defaulters) Directions, 2025, respectively,
shall continue to be applicable in cases where a bank enter into compromise
settlement with such borrowers, and the cooling periods specified in paragraphs 26
and 27, in respect of such borrowers, shall be without prejudice to such penal
measures.
requirements.object: in cases where a bank enter into compromise settlement with such borrowers, and the cooling periods specified in paragraphs

requirements.full_sentence:
As long as larger policy concerns are suitably addressed and the costs of malafide
actions are made to be borne by the perpetrators, early recoveries by a bank should
be a preferred option, subject to safeguards.

The compromise settlements with the borrowers under these Directions shall be
without prejudice to the provisions of any other statute in force.
requirements.object: the provisions of any other statute in force

requirements.full_sentence:
The monetary ceiling of cases referred to the Lok Adalats organised by Civil Courts
for compromise settlements shall be ₹20 lakh.

In addition to the requirement under paragraph 30, wherever a bank had
commenced recovery proceedings under a judicial forum and the same is pending
before such judicial forum, any settlement arrived at with the borrower shall be
subject to obtaining a consent decree from the concerned judicial authorities.
requirements.object: a consent decree from the concerned judicial authorities

requirements.full_sentence:
The instructions contained in this Chapter shall be applicable to resolution of
exposures of borrowers impacted by a natural calamity or, mutatis mutandis,
exposures of borrowers impacted by external events such as riots / disturbances
that result in loss to economic activity (hereinafter collectively referred to as
'calamity'), upon the declaration of such calamity by the Central / State
Governments (in accordance with the framework placed by the concerned
Government for this purpose).
requirements.object: of exposures of borrowers impacted by a natural calamity or, mutatis mutandis, exposures of borrowers impacted by external

conditions:
where relief measures have
already been provided as on the effective date of these Amendment Directions
requirements.full_sentence:
These instructions shall not apply to borrower accounts where relief measures have
already been provided as on the effective date of these Amendment Directions,
and such accounts shall continue to be guided by the existing prudential
guidelines.
requirements.object: where relief measures have already been provided as on the effective date of these Amendment Directions, and such accounts

requirements.full_sentence:
However, any fresh resolution in such accounts subsequent to the
effective date of these Amendment Directions shall be as per the guidelines
contained in this Chapter.
requirements.object: guidelines contained in this Chapter

requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of resolution under this Chapter, bank shall be guided by the
principles enshrined in the Reserve Bank of India (Small Finance Banks -
Resolution of Stressed Assets) Directions, 2025 dated November 28, 2025.
requirements.object: principles enshrined in the Reserve Bank of India (Small Finance Banks - Resolution of Stressed Assets) Directions, 2025 dated Novemb

conditions:
if a larger part of the State / Union Territory (UT)
has been affected
requirements.full_sentence:
Upon declaration of a calamity, if a larger part of the State / Union Territory (UT)
has been affected, the SLBC / UTLBC convenor bank shall convene a special
SLBC / UTLBC meeting within 15 days of such declaration.
requirements.object: / UTLBC meeting within 15 days of such declaration

conditions: If the calamity has affected only a part of the State/ UT
requirements.full_sentence:
If the calamity has affected only a part of the State/ UT, the convenor of the DCC
of the affected district(s) shall convene the meeting within 15 days of such
declaration, after due consultation with the SLBC / UTLBC convenor.
requirements.object: 15 days of such declaration, after due consultation with the SLBC / UTLBC convenor

requirements.full_sentence:
In the special SLBC / UTLBC / DCC meeting, the position of the affected areas may
be assessed in terms of the severity of the impact of the calamity on the economic
6 Inserted with effect from July 01, 2026 vide Reserve Bank of India (Rural Cooperative Banks - Resolution of Stressed Assets) Amendment Directions, 2026 dated April 29, 2026.
requirements.object: of the severity of the impact of the calamity on the economic 6 Inserted with effect from July 01, 2026 vide Reserve Bank of Indi

requirements.full_sentence:
SLBC / UTLBC /
DCC may also formulate the objective criteria for identifying impacted borrowers,
and the extent of moratorium period, if any.
requirements.object: criteria for identifying impacted borrowers, and the extent of moratorium period

requirements.full_sentence:
The decisions taken in the special SLBC / UTLBC meeting(s) as mentioned at
paragraph 33F above, shall be conveyed by the SLBC / UTLBC convenor bank
immediately to all SLBC / UTLBC members along with the minutes of the meeting.
requirements.object: SLBC / UTLBC convenor bank immediately to all SLBC / UTLBC members along with the minutes of the meeting

requirements.full_sentence:
A copy of the same shall also be forwarded by the SLBC / UTLBC convenor bank
to the respective Regional Office of Reserve Bank and the Non-Banking Financial
Companies (NBFCs) / Urban Co-operative Banks (UCBs) operating in the area.
requirements.object: the SLBC / UTLBC convenor bank to the respective Regional Office of Reserve Bank and the Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs)

requirements.full_sentence:
The decisions taken in the special DCC meeting(s) shall be conveyed by the DCC
convenor bank immediately to the DCC members and the SLBC / UTLBC along
with the minutes of the meeting.
requirements.object: DCC convenor bank immediately to the DCC members and the SLBC / UTLBC along with the minutes of the meeting

requirements.full_sentence:
A copy of the same shall also be forwarded by
the DCC convenor bank to the respective Regional Office of Reserve Bank and
the NBFCs / UCBs operating in the area.
requirements.object: the DCC convenor bank to the respective Regional Office of Reserve Bank and the NBFCs / UCBs operating in the area

requirements.full_sentence:
The decisions taken in the special SLBC / UTLBC / DCC meeting(s) shall be given
adequate publicity by SLBCs / UTLBC / DCCs / banks through various methods
such as brochures, banners, advertisement in newspapers, visits by field staff,
and other suitable modes, for the benefit of affected borrowers.
requirements.object: by SLBCs / UTLBC / DCCs / banks through various methods such as brochures, banners, advertisement in newspapers, visits by

requirements.full_sentence:
Where the SLBC / UTLBC / DCC, as the case may be, recommends extending relief
measures to the impacted borrowers, banks may implement resolution plans in
respect of such borrowers in terms of the provisions of this Chapter.
requirements.object: measures to the impacted borrowers, banks may implement resolution plans in respect of such borrowers in terms of the

conditions:
In case no specific date of occurrence is ascertainable in respect of
a calamity
requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: In case no specific date of occurrence is ascertainable in respect of
a calamity, the date of declaration of such calamity by the Central / State
Governments shall be treated as the date of occurrence.
requirements.object: date of occurrence

requirements.full_sentence:
Borrowers, whose loan accounts do not fulfil the required eligibility conditions for
resolution under this Chapter may continue to be considered for resolution under
other provisions of these Directions.
requirements.object: for resolution under other provisions of these Directions

requirements.full_sentence: The provisions of this Chapter shall not apply to the refinance portfolio of a bank.
requirements.object: portfolio of a bank

requirements.full_sentence:
Those borrowers shall be eligible for resolution under this Chapter whose accounts
are classified as 'Standard', but which are not in default for more than 30 days
with the bank in respect of any of their facilities, as on the date of occurrence of
the calamity.
requirements.object: under this Chapter whose accounts are classified as 'Standard', but which are not in default for more than 30 days with the

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank may decide on participating in a particular DRS notified by a Government,
based on its Board approved policy, subject to the extant regulatory norms.
requirements.object: a particular DRS notified by a Government, based on its Board approved policy

requirements.full_sentence:
Any provision of the scheme that may warrant modification in long term interest of
the borrowers or for prudential reasons may be duly brought to the notice of the
concerned authority/ies through the State Level Bankers' Committee / District level
Consultative Committee, during the consultation phase while designing the DRS.
requirements.object: term interest of the borrowers or for prudential reasons may be duly brought to the notice of the concerned authority/ies

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall clearly determine the eventual outstanding that may crystallise in their
books in respect of the borrowers proposed to be covered under the DRS, including
the accumulated interest in non-performing accounts, by the time the dues are
settled under the DRS, to enable the Government to suitably arrange for the extent
of fiscal participation.
requirements.object: outstanding that may crystallise in their books in respect of the borrowers proposed to be covered under the DRS, inclu

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall ensure that the borrowers to be covered under DRS are selected strictly
as per terms of such schemes so as to avoid subsequent non-admission by the
authorities on technical grounds.
requirements.object: to be covered under DRS are selected strictly as per terms of such schemes so as to avoid subsequent non-admission by the au

requirements.full_sentence:
The terms and conditions of the scheme as well as the prudential aspects, including
cooling period for extending fresh credit, impact on credit score etc., shall be clearly
communicated to the borrowers at the time of obtaining explicit consent from the
borrower for availing benefits under a proposed DRS.
requirements.object: the borrowers at the time of obtaining explicit consent from the borrower for availing benefits under a proposed DRS

requirements.full_sentence:
Any waiver of accrued but unrealised interest and/ or sacrifice of principal
undertaken by a bank in the borrower accounts of beneficiaries of the DRS, either
as part of the implementation of the scheme or subsequent to its implementation,
shall be treated as a compromise settlement and shall attract the prudential
treatment contained in paragraphs 17 to 32.
requirements.object: compromise settlement and shall attract the prudential treatment contained in paragraphs 17 to 32

conditions:
If the funds received by a bank as part of the DRS covers the entire outstanding
dues of the borrower
requirements.full_sentence:
If the funds received by a bank as part of the DRS covers the entire outstanding
dues of the borrower, including principal and interest accrued till the date of receipt
<image>
of funds by the bank, the same shall lead to extinguishment of borrower's debt
obligations.
requirements.object: borrower's debt obligations

conditions:
where the funds received by a bank as part of the scheme are not adequate
to cover the entire outstanding dues of the borrower
requirements.full_sentence:
In cases where the funds received by a bank as part of the scheme are not adequate
to cover the entire outstanding dues of the borrower, leading to residual exposure
(principal and / or accrued interest), the asset classification of the residual exposure
shall be evaluated as per the terms and conditions of the original loan contract.
requirements.object: the terms and conditions of the original loan contract

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that any changes / modifications to the terms and conditions of the original
loan contract in such cases shall be evaluated against the test of restructuring as
defined in these Directions and shall attract the prudential treatment therein.
requirements.object: test of restructuring as defined in these Directions and shall attract the prudential treatment therein

requirements.full_sentence:
Any fresh credit exposure to such borrowers shall be as per the commercial
discretion of the bank under relevant internal policy, subject to extant applicable
regulations.
requirements.object: commercial discretion of the bank under relevant internal policy

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank's reporting in respect of the borrowers under the scheme to the credit
information companies shall be guided by the extant guidelines in this regard.
requirements.object: extant guidelines in this regard

requirements.full_sentence:
There shall not be creation of any receivable against the Government on account of
the DRS and the exposure shall continue to be on the borrower till receipt of funds
by the bank.
requirements.object: receivable against the Government on account of the DRS and the exposure shall continue to be on the borrower till receipt of funds

requirements.full_sentence:
Till receipt of funds, a bank shall continue to apply the prudential norms including
prudential norms on income recognition, asset classification and provisioning, and
wherever the accounts are non-performing, the bank may pursue recovery measures
as per their Board approved policy against such borrowers.
requirements.object: prudential norms including prudential norms on income recognition, asset classification and provisioning, and wherever the accou

requirements.full_sentence:
The instructions contained in paragraphs 34 to 45 shall apply in respect of DRS
notified on or after December 31, 2024 and shall be without prejudice to the
instructions on resolution of stressed assets contained in these Directions.
requirements.object: DRS notified on or after December 31, 2024 and shall be without prejudice to the instructions on resolution of stressed assets con

requirements.full_sentence:
In respect of relief measures announced prior to December 31, 2024, any dues
pending receipt from Government, for more than 90 days shall attract specific
provision of 100%.

A bank shall take necessary action and actively follow up with the respective
Governments for settlement of dues referred to in paragraph 48.
requirements.object: actively follow up with the respective Governments for settlement of dues referred to in paragraph 48

requirements.full_sentence:
To mitigate the burden of debt servicing brought about by trade disruptions caused
by global headwinds and to ensure the continuity of viable businesses, banks may
extend relief measures to eligible borrowers, as specified under Reserve Bank of
India (Trade Relief Measures) Directions, 2025 dated November 14, 2025.
requirements.object: eligible borrowers, as specified under Reserve Bank of India (Trade Relief Measures) Directions, 2025 dated November 14, 202

requirements.full_sentence:
The directions,
instructions and guidelines already repealed vide any of the directions, instructions,
and guidelines listed in the above notification shall continue to remain repealed.

Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken, or
initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall continue to
be governed by the provisions thereof.
requirements.object: by the provisions thereof

requirements.full_sentence:
All approvals or acknowledgments granted
under these repealed lists shall be deemed as governed by these Directions.
requirements.object: by these Directions
affect: (1) any right, obligation or liability acquired, accrued, or incurred thereunder

requirements.full_sentence:
and any such investigation, legal proceedings or remedy may be instituted,
continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may
be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guidelines had not been
repealed.
requirements.object: enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guideline

requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation of
the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being
in force.
requirements.object: and not in derogation of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being in force

requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order to
remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of these
Directions, the Reserve Bank may, if it considers necessary, issue necessary
clarifications in respect of any matter covered herein and the interpretation of any
provision of these Directions given by the Reserve Bank shall be final and binding.

For the purpose of the Model Operating Procedure (MOP), Debt Relief Schemes (DRS)
refer to Schemes notified by the State Governments that entail funding by the fiscal
authorities to cover debt obligations of a targeted segment of borrowers that the regulated
entities are required to sacrifice / waive.

Announcement / notification of any such DRS should include the specific stress or
distress situation necessitating announcement of such support.
requirements.object: or distress situation necessitating announcement of such support

requirements.full_sentence:
Given the broader
implications of such DRS for the credit culture, while broad based relief measures can be
addressed through pure fiscal support in the form of Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT), DRS
should be considered only as a measure of last resort when other measures to alleviate
financial stress have failed.
requirements.object: a measure of last resort when other measures to alleviate financial stress have failed

requirements.full_sentence:
Before announcing any DRS, Governments may engage with the State Level Bankers'
Committee (SLBC)/ District level Consultative Committee (DCC) to evolve a coordinated
action plan for conceptualisation, design, and implementation of the DRS.
requirements.object: Level Bankers' Committee (SLBC)/ District level Consultative Committee (DCC) to evolve a coordinated action plan for conceptuali

requirements.full_sentence:
The schemes
should, cover critical aspects of the scheme like identification of borrowers, impact
assessment, implementation timelines, resolution of issues concerning settlement of dues
by Government to the lending institutions, etc.
requirements.object: of the scheme like identification of borrowers, impact assessment, implementation timelines, resolution of issues concerning

requirements.full_sentence:
The design features should ensure that the DRS do not impact the financial stability
aspects of the region / State or create moral hazards in the borrower segments.
requirements.object: do not impact the financial stability aspects of the region / State or create moral hazards in the borrower segments

requirements.full_sentence: should also be taken into account.
requirements.object: account

requirements.full_sentence:
Detailed budgetary provisions / funding may be provided upfront towards any proposed
DRS to fully cover the required settlement amounts.
requirements.object: any proposed DRS to fully cover the required settlement amounts

conditions:
Where regulated entities have dues
<image>
from the Government
requirements.full_sentence:
Where regulated entities have dues
<image>
from the Government, pertaining to earlier DRS schemes, new schemes should be
announced only on a fully pre-funded basis.
requirements.object: a fully pre-funded basis

requirements.full_sentence:
The DRS should be targeted only at the impacted borrowers and should not contain
any restrictive covenant against timely repayments.
requirements.object: the impacted borrowers and should not contain any restrictive covenant against timely repayments

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, it should specify the criteria
for determining eligible borrowers on an objective basis, detailed timeline of critical /
material events, including cut-off dates for filing/ submission, acknowledgement, approval
and settlement of claims along with compensation clauses for delays in settling the funds,
on part of the Government.
requirements.object: determining eligible borrowers on an objective basis, detailed timeline of critical / material events, including cut-off date

requirements.full_sentence:
The DRS should cover the entire outstanding dues of the borrowers being covered,
including principal and accumulated interest till the date of receipt of funds by the
regulated entities from the Government.
requirements.object: dues of the borrowers being covered, including principal and accumulated interest till the date of receipt of funds by th

requirements.full_sentence:
The DRS should not require the creation of a receivable in the books of the regulated
entity against the Government.
requirements.object: a receivable in the books of the regulated entity against the Government

requirements.full_sentence:
The exposure of regulated entities to the borrower shall
continue and shall be reduced to the extent of funds received from the Government.
requirements.object: reduced to the extent of funds received from the Government

requirements.full_sentence:
The entire implementation of the Scheme and settlement of claims by the Governments
to the regulated entities, should generally be completed within 45 to 60 days.
requirements.object: 45 to 60 days

requirements.full_sentence:
The DRS should not contain any provision contrary to any regulatory instruction issued
by the Reserve Bank / NABARD.
requirements.object: to any regulatory instruction issued by the Reserve Bank / NABARD

requirements.full_sentence:
The design of the DRS should not contain any provision that casts any obligations on
the regulated entities, directly or indirectly, to:
a.
requirements.object: casts any obligations on the regulated entities, directly or indirectly, to: a

conditions:
if the regulated entities agree to any of the above at the time of design of DRS
or subsequently
requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
However, if the regulated entities agree to any of the above at the time of design of DRS
or subsequently, as per their Board-approved policies, it shall be subject to the applicable
prudential guidelines.
requirements.object: applicable prudential guidelines
